references.hebrew: גַּם־אִ֤ישׁ שְׁלוֹמִ֨י ׀ אֲשֶׁר־בָּטַ֣חְתִּי ב֭וֹ אוֹכֵ֣ל לַחְמִ֑י הִגְדִּ֖יל עָלַ֣י עָקֵֽב׃
references.english:
My ally in whom I trusted,
even someone who shares my bread,
has been utterly false to me.chas been utterly false to me Meaning of Heb. uncertain.

references.english:
But when I stumble, they gleefully gather;
wretches gather against me,
I know not why;
they tear at me without end.

references.english: David entreated God for the boy; David fasted, and he went in and spent the night lyingdlying Some Septuagint mss. and 4QSamᵃ add “in sackcloth”; cf. 1 Kings 21.27. on the ground. I am weary with groaning;
every night I drench my bed,
I melt my couch in tears.

Who can be aware of errors?
Clear me of unperceived guilt,

and from willful sinsdwillful sins Or “the arrogant”; cf. 119.51. keep Your servant;

Can anyone rake embers into their bosom

Purge me with hyssop till I am pure;
wash me till I am whiter than snow.

Let me again rejoice in Your help;
let a vigorous spirit sustain me.

May those who fear You,
those who know Your decrees,
turn again to me.

Probe me, O ETERNAL One, and try me,

You have visited me at night, probed my mind,
You have tested me and found nothing amiss;
I determined that my mouth should not transgress.

For the leader. Of David.
In GOD I take refuge;
how can you say to me,

Against You alone have I sinned,
and done what is evil in Your sight;
so You are just in Your sentence,
and right in Your judgment.

For I am on the verge of collapse;
my pain is always with me.

references.english: So the ETERNAL God cast a deep sleep upon the Human; and, while he slept, [God] took one of his sidesgsides So Septuagint and Ibn Ezra; trad. “ribs.” and closed up the flesh at that site.
